evals.prompt: Map the end-to-end customer journey for a B2B SaaS project management tool. The product is used by teams (5-50 people) for task tracking, collaboration, and reporting. Key stages would be: Awareness, Trial, Purchase, Onboarding, Active Usage, Renewal. What are the critical touchpoints and pain points we should map?
evals.expected_output: Should outline journey stages with major touchpoints: (1) Awareness - website, product hunt, competitor comparisons, free content; (2) Trial - signup, guided onboarding, first project creation, team invite; (3) Purchase - pricing page, sales conversation, contract; (4) Onboarding - account setup, team setup, first task creation, first collaboration; (5) Active usage - daily usage, feature discovery, support interactions; (6) Renewal - value demonstration, expansion conversation, renewal contract. Should identify likely pain points: confusing signup process, unclear value in trial, overly complex onboarding, difficulty finding features, poor support responsiveness, pricing uncertainty. Should recommend mapping emotions (confusion during signup, delight on first collaboration, anxiety about new features) and identifying moments of truth (first successful team collaboration, first report generation).

evals.prompt: We see a big NPS drop at the 90-day renewal point for our SaaS product. Active users have NPS 50+; renewals at 90 days have NPS 30. What root cause analysis would you do to diagnose this?
evals.expected_output: Should recommend structured root cause analysis: (1) Segment the data - do all customer types drop, or specific segments? (2) Compare workflows - what are high-NPS customers doing differently at 90 days vs. low-NPS? (3) Review support interactions - are there common issues reported by customers who churn? (4) Analyze feature usage - are customers using the right features? Or struggling with features needed for their workflow? (5) Check communication patterns - have we engaged the customer at 60-day mark? Or do they feel abandoned? (6) Interview churned customers - ask directly: 'Why are you considering switching?' Likely root causes: (a) Haven't achieved business value needed to justify renewal, (b) Encountered a workflow blocker they can't resolve, (c) Competitors launched new feature they need, (d) Support responsiveness is poor, (e) Pricing increase feels unjustified. Should recommend mapping the 60-90 day touchpoints and identifying where value realization breaks down.

evals.prompt: Create a service blueprint for customer support operations. We have a support team (15 people) handling tickets across email, chat, and phone. Customers report slow response times and inconsistent answers. What should the blueprint show?
evals.expected_output: Should map: (1) Frontstage (customer-visible): Customer submits ticket → Receives acknowledgment → Gets response → Submits follow-up → Issue resolved. (2) Touchpoints: Email, chat, phone, help center. (3) Backstage processes: Ticket triage → Assignment to specialist → Investigation/resolution → Response delivery → Customer verification. (4) Support systems: Ticketing platform (Zendesk), knowledge base, internal docs. (5) Teams: Tier 1 (triage, FAQ answers), Tier 2 (technical resolution), Tier 3 (escalations, architectural issues). (6) Pain points: Inconsistent answers likely due to knowledge siloing (not all reps have same docs), slow response due to no SLA/prioritization, no feedback loop to improve common issues. (7) Improvement opportunities: Create shared knowledge base with decision trees per issue type, implement SLA targets with prioritization, create feedback loop to product for common issues, cross-train tier 1 on tier 2 issues, measure quality/speed per rep.

evals.prompt: Our onboarding process for a design tool currently takes 3 weeks from signup to first completed project. Customers report confusion about workflows and features. What's the redesigned journey that gets users to first success in 3 days?
evals.expected_output: Should redesign journey from linear to goal-based: (1) Current (linear): Signup → System tour → Feature tutorials → [customer struggles to find first task]. (2) Redesigned (goal-based): Signup → 'What will you design first?' [goal selection] → Guided setup for that specific workflow → Create first design → First success moment. (3) Specific changes: Replace generic features tour with goal-specific guidance; create multiple onboarding paths based on user type (marketing → branding work; product → UI design; etc.); reduce tour length from 3 weeks to 3 days by focusing on first success workflow only; create follow-up tutorials for advanced features triggered by usage patterns (not mandatory tours). (4) Touchpoints: Interactive goal selection on signup, in-app guided experience, email checkpoints at key moments (project created, first share), help chat for questions. (5) Success metric: Time to first completed project, reduced from 3 weeks to 3 days.

evals.prompt: Identify moments of truth across an e-commerce shopping journey. These are critical moments where we win or lose trust. For an online fashion retailer, what are the 3 most critical moments?
evals.expected_output: Should identify moments where customer sentiment shifts dramatically: (1) Product page to add-to-cart: 'Does this product look like the photos? Will it fit me?' Critical signals: detailed photos (multiple angles, on models), honest reviews, clear sizing information, return policy visible. Failure here = high cart abandonment. (2) Checkout to order confirmation: 'Is my payment safe? Is this being shipped quickly?' Critical signals: trust badges (security, payment logos), clear shipping timeline, transparent costs (no surprise fees), easy returns. Failure = abandonment. (3) Delivery/unboxing to satisfaction: 'Does it match the product page? Can I return if I don't like it?' Critical signals: easy return process, good packaging, quality matches description, return label included. Failure = returns, chargebacks, bad reviews. Should recommend mapping these three moments in detail: What does customer see/feel? What could go wrong? How can we improve?

evals.prompt: We want to align product, marketing, and support teams around the customer journey. What would you include in a cross-functional workshop to build shared understanding?
evals.expected_output: Should structure a 3-hour workshop: (1) Context setting (15 min): Share research findings on customer pain points and churn. (2) Journey walkthrough (30 min): Present the customer journey map stage by stage. Ask each team: 'What's our role here? What could go better?' (3) Pain point identification (20 min): For each major stage, brainstorm what's broken. Capture in affinity map. (4) Team role clarity (20 min): Map which team owns each stage and touchpoint. Identify gaps/overlaps. (5) Opportunity prioritization (20 min): Identify 3-5 high-impact improvements; estimate effort and ownership. (6) Action planning (15 min): Assign owners; set next steps. Outputs: Shared journey map, identified pain points, prioritized improvements, clear ownership, cross-functional alignment.

evals.prompt: Compare the journey experience of your highest-retention customers (stay 3+ years) vs. highest-churn customers (leave within 6 months). What differences would you look for?
evals.expected_output: Should compare across multiple dimensions: (1) Onboarding: Do retained customers have guided onboarding? Do churned customers get lost? (2) First success: Do retained customers achieve measurable value quickly? Do churned customers give up before seeing value? (3) Feature adoption: Do retained customers use multiple features or just one? Do churned customers use only surface-level features? (4) Support engagement: Do retained customers get help when stuck? Do churned customers have support issues go unresolved? (5) Communication: Do retained customers get check-ins, upsells, new feature announcements? Do churned customers feel neglected? (6) Workflow fit: Do retained customers find their workflow in the product? Do churned customers have to change how they work? (7) Community/advocacy: Do retained customers participate in community, refer others? Do churned customers feel isolated? Should recommend building separate journeys for each segment and identifying highest-leverage improvements to move churned segment behavior toward retained segment behavior.

evals.prompt: We have high satisfaction with product features but low NPS overall. Customers love the tool but are churning at renewal. What could be the disconnects between feature satisfaction and overall retention?
evals.expected_output: Should identify disconnects: (1) Feature satisfaction ≠ business value: Customers love using the tool but haven't solved their core problem or realized ROI; they churn because they can't justify the cost. (2) Onboarding gap: Customers appreciate features once they know how to use them, but poor onboarding means they never get there without support. (3) Workflow mismatch: Features are good, but the workflow doesn't match how customers actually work; they end up using workarounds and feel frustrated. (4) Support perception: Customers love features but had bad support experience that taints overall perception. (5) Pricing disconnect: Customers like the tool but feel it's overpriced relative to value; they shop competitors at renewal. (6) Expansion failure: Customers are happy with free/basic tier but never upgrade because value of paid features isn't clear. Should recommend: (1) Survey churned customers specifically on renewal decision, (2) Track business value metrics alongside feature satisfaction, (3) Map support interactions to NPS and identify support-driven churn, (4) Align pricing to demonstrated value, (5) Create clearer expansion path showing value of paid features.
